paper_title: A tight Erdős-Pósa function for wheel minors
summary: Conjecture 1.4 from arXiv:1710.06282 — that every graph of treewidth at least f(r)·k·log(k+1) has k vertex-disjoint subgraphs each of treewidth at least r — remains open as of May 2026. No resolution was found in the indexed literature; the conjecture is a treewidth-level analogue of the more general planar-H Erdős-Pósa conjecture (Conjecture 1.2 of the same paper), and is implied by it via the r×r grid. The Chekuri–Chuzhoy framework (arXiv:1304.1577) achieves a weaker c′≥1 factor but does not attain the tight k log(k+1) bound asserted here.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch of the abstract confirms this paper addresses a different conjecture (Aboulker, Adler, Kim, Sintiari, Trotignon) about unavoidable induced subgraphs of bounded-degree graphs with large treewidth, not Conjecture 1.4 of arXiv:1710.06282. The candidate match appears to be a false positive.

WebFetch of the abstract confirms this paper is about chordality, χ-boundedness of intersection graph classes, and Gyárfás-type questions; it does not mention the treewidth decomposition conjecture of arXiv:1710.06282. The candidate match is a false positive.

notes: No follow-up paper was found that proves, disproves, or makes substantial partial progress on Conjecture 1.4 specifically. The conjecture is implied by the broader Conjecture 1.2 (Erdős-Pósa for any fixed planar H), which also appears to remain open. The two internal-corpus candidate references (2108.01162 and 2404.05992) are false positives from fuzzy matching and do not address this conjecture.
